name: 🔴 Red Team
prompts: Enable 【Red Team Mode】. Your task is to act as a competitor and harsh user, ruthlessly review the following proposal. List at least 5 critical logic flaws, risks or disasters. Do not spare feelings. Content below:

scenarioPrompts.product: Enable 【Red Team Mode】. As a competitor and harsh user, ruthlessly review this product proposal. List at least 5 critical logic flaws, market risks or UX disasters. Content below:
scenarioPrompts.code: Enable 【Red Team Mode】. As a security expert, review the following code. Focus only on vulnerabilities (injection, overflow), concurrency risks and performance failure points. Try to 'attack' this code. Code below:
scenarioPrompts.decision: Enable 【Red Team Mode】. As the opposition, run a 'premortem' on this decision. Assume it fails completely in 1 year; backtrace root causes and blind spots. Decision below:
scenarioPrompts.learning: Enable 【Red Team Mode】. For this claim or concept, play devil's advocate: give the strongest counterarguments, counterexamples and fallacy charges. Content below:

name: ⚛️ First Principles
prompts: Use 【First Principles】 thinking. Strip all analogies and conventions, return to fundamental facts, and re-derive the solution. Content below:

scenarioPrompts.product: Use 【First Principles】. Ignore existing competitors and industry norms; break this product need into basic user desires and physical/technical limits, then reconstruct the solution from zero. Need below:
scenarioPrompts.code: Use 【First Principles】. From the basics of computation, storage and network cost, analyze the optimal approach to this technical problem. Problem below:
scenarioPrompts.decision: Use 【First Principles】. Strip 'everyone does it' analogies; return to the core economic or logical facts and re-derive our decision basis. Decision below:
scenarioPrompts.learning: Use 【First Principles】. Break this concept into irreducible axioms or definitions, then derive step by step how it holds. Concept below:

name: 👶 Feynman Technique
description: Explain simply; if you can't, you don't understand
prompts: Use the 【Feynman Technique】. Explain this complex idea as if to a 5-year-old. If you can't explain it simply, you don't understand it yet. Content below:

scenarioPrompts.product: Use the 【Feynman Technique】. Assume your audience is an 80-year-old with no tech background; explain this product's core value and how to use it with plain analogies. Content below:
scenarioPrompts.code: Use the 【Feynman Technique】. Explain this code to a junior intern without jargon; use clear diagrams or metaphors so the logic is obvious. Code below:
scenarioPrompts.decision: Use the 【Feynman Technique】. Turn this strategic decision into a simple story so any employee can understand why it matters. Decision below:
scenarioPrompts.learning: Use the 【Feynman Technique】. Explain this concept as if to a 5-year-old. If you can't, you don't understand it yet. Concept below:

name: 🏗️ Architecture Review
prompts: Review the following from an 【architecture perspective】. Assess structure, dependencies and future scalability. Content below:

scenarioPrompts.product: Review this product design from a 【system architecture】 view. Assess coupling between modules, over-engineering, and bottlenecks when extending. Design below:
scenarioPrompts.code: Review this code from a 【software architecture】 view. Assess SOLID adherence, module boundaries and scalability under load. Code below:
scenarioPrompts.decision: Review this decision from an 【organizational architecture】 view. Assess impact on processes, collaboration and resources; single points of failure or over-reliance on roles. Decision below:
scenarioPrompts.learning: Build a 【knowledge architecture】 for this topic: core concepts, relationships and dependencies. Topic below:

name: 🛡️ Security Audit
prompts: Act as 【Security Auditor】. Review the following for risks, vulnerabilities and abuse potential. Content below:

scenarioPrompts.product: Act as 【Security Auditor】 for this product. Analyze abuse risks (fraud, spam, harassment) and privacy leakage. Description below:
scenarioPrompts.code: Act as 【Security Auditor】. Check for OWASP Top 10 (XSS, SQL injection, IDOR, etc.) and suggest fixes. Code below:
scenarioPrompts.decision: Run a 【Risk Audit】. Assess legal, reputational and data-security risks of this decision. Decision below:
scenarioPrompts.learning: Explain 【Security mechanisms】. For this technical principle, explain trust model, crypto and attack surface. Content below:

name: 🧠 Deep Reasoning
prompts: Enable 【Deep Reasoning Mode】. Reason step by step. Before each conclusion, reflect on its validity, explore at least 3 alternatives, then give the best answer. Content below:

scenarioPrompts.product: Enable 【Deep Reasoning Mode】. For this product challenge, reason step by step; reflect before each conclusion, explore 3+ options, then conclude. Challenge below:
scenarioPrompts.code: Enable 【Deep Reasoning Mode】. Simulate how this code behaves under stress (concurrency, latency, low memory); trace race conditions and failures. Code below:
scenarioPrompts.decision: Enable 【Deep Reasoning Mode】. Run second- and third-order effects: if we do this, what happens next? Build the causal chain. Decision below:
scenarioPrompts.learning: Enable 【Deep Reasoning Mode】. Derive this theory like a proof: fill every gap, verify every assumption. Content below:

name: 🔍 Meta-Cognition Check
description: Force AI to assess its own answer quality
prompts: Enable 【Meta-Cognition Check】. Review your answer (or upcoming answer): does it meet the current thinking-mode bar? Any hallucination, pleasing or logic leaps? Self-correct if needed. Content below:

scenarioPrompts.product: Enable 【Meta-Cognition Check】. Does this product plan drift from the original user pain? Are we self-indulging? Point out assumption bias. Plan below:
scenarioPrompts.code: Enable 【Meta-Cognition Check】. Did we sacrifice quality for speed? Any 'we'll fix it later' that is actually debt? Code below:
scenarioPrompts.decision: Enable 【Meta-Cognition Check】. Did cognitive bias (confirmation, sunk cost) affect this decision? Is the reasoning objective? Decision below:
scenarioPrompts.learning: Enable 【Meta-Cognition Check】. Am I memorizing or understanding? Am I avoiding the hard core? Content below:

name: 📈 Deep Upgrade
description: Reject shallow answers, dig for root logic
prompts: Enable 【Deep Upgrade Mode】. The previous answer was too shallow; dig for deeper logic and causality, go three levels deeper. Content below:

scenarioPrompts.product: Enable 【Deep Upgrade Mode】. Go beyond features: sociology, psychology and ecosystem position of this product. Content below:
scenarioPrompts.code: Enable 【Deep Upgrade Mode】. Beyond syntax: explain at OS, compiler or hardware level what this code really does. Code below:
scenarioPrompts.decision: Enable 【Deep Upgrade Mode】. Extend the timeline to 10 years and the scope to macro environment; re-evaluate this decision. Decision below:
scenarioPrompts.learning: Enable 【Deep Upgrade Mode】. Don't just say 'what'; say 'why' and 'how it evolved'; build an evolution tree. Content below:

name: 🛠️ Product Polisher
description: Empathy map → Red team → First principles
prompts: Run 【Product Polisher】. 1. Empathy map: user pains; 2. Red team: find flaws; 3. First principles: rebuild the best solution. Execute in order. Content below:

name: 🧭 Decision Compass
description: Second-order effects → Opportunity cost → Scenario planning
prompts: Run 【Decision Compass】. 1. Second-order effects; 2. Opportunity cost; 3. Three future scenarios. Execute in order. Content below:

name: 💡 Idea to Action
prompts: Run 【Idea to Action】. 1. SCAMPER brainstorm: 10+ ideas; 2. First-principles filter: essential solution; 3. Structured execution plan. Execute in order. Content below:

name: 🎓 Learning Accelerator
prompts: Run 【Learning Accelerator】. 1. Feynman: explain core concept; 2. Knowledge graph: structure; 3. Assume my mistakes and correct them. Execute in order. Content below:

name: 🛡️ Code Fortress
prompts: Run 【Code Fortress】. 1. Logic review: bugs and smell; 2. Security scan: vulnerabilities; 3. Performance improvements. Execute in order. Content below:

name: 🧠 Reasoning Max
prompts: Run 【Reasoning Max】. 1. Generate 3–5 independent reasoning paths; 2. Self-consistency vote; 3. Reflection loop to correct. Output the best answer. Content below:

name: 🤖 Agent Orchestrator
description: Task split → Tool plan → Result merge
prompts: Run 【Agent Orchestrator】. 1. Split task into sub-agents; 2. Plan tools per agent; 3. Merge outputs. Simulate step by step. Content below:

name: 🚀 Lyra Protocol
prompts: Start Lyra mode: break down my intent, find ambiguities, ask clarifying questions, then deliver. Content below:

scenarioPrompts.product: Lyra product mode: use 4-D (Discover, Define, Design, Deliver). Don't give a solution first; ask 3–5 key questions about user, pain, value. Then deliver. Content below:
scenarioPrompts.code: Lyra tech mode: act as senior architect. Don't write code first; ask about performance, scale, stack, edge cases. Turn fuzzy needs into specs. Content below:
scenarioPrompts.decision: Lyra decision mode: clarify via Socratic questions—goals, opportunity cost, risks. Build a decision matrix. Content below:
scenarioPrompts.learning: Lyra teaching mode: test my current level, find gaps, then tailor a learning path. Don't lecture first. Topic below:

name: 🌱 Ideation & Divergence
description: Break mental sets, generate many options
prompts: Enable 【Brainstorm Mode】. Use SCAMPER; propose 10 disruptive ideas. The crazier the better. Content below:

scenarioPrompts.product: Enable 【Brainstorm Mode】. For this product feature, use SCAMPER to propose 10 disruptive ideas. Content below:
scenarioPrompts.code: Enable 【Refactor Storm】. For this code, propose 5 different refactor/optimization directions (algorithm, pattern, data structure). Code below:
scenarioPrompts.decision: Enable 【Lateral Thinking】. For this decision, offer 5 unconventional alternatives. Decision below:
scenarioPrompts.learning: Enable 【Analogy Mode】. Connect this concept to unrelated domains (biology, architecture, strategy) for cross-domain insight. Concept below:

name: 🎭 Role Play
prompts: Play 【relevant expert】. Answer in that role's tone and depth. Content below:

scenarioPrompts.product: Play 【Jobs-style PM】. Review this product with minimalism and perfectionism; criticize excess and poor UX. Content below:
scenarioPrompts.code: Play 【Linux kernel maintainer】. Review this code for memory, pointers, efficiency; strict style. Code below:
scenarioPrompts.decision: Play 【Warren Buffett】. Evaluate this decision with value-investing lens: moat, long-term compounding, margin of safety. Decision below:
scenarioPrompts.learning: Play 【Feynman】. Explain this theory with humor, intuition and vivid analogies. Content below:

name: 🔮 Foresight
prompts: Enable 【Scenario Planning】. Simulate multiple futures and build resilient strategy. Content below:

scenarioPrompts.product: Enable 【Scenario Planning】. In 3 years the market shifts (tech, regulation, disruption); describe 3 futures and this product's fit. Content below:
scenarioPrompts.code: Enable 【Tech Evolution】. If traffic grows 100x or a dependency is abandoned, what breaks? Propose evolution. Code below:
scenarioPrompts.decision: Enable 【Second-Order Effects】. Trace direct, second- and third-order effects of this decision. Decision below:
scenarioPrompts.learning: Enable 【Future Skills】. Given AI trends, what part of this skill stays valuable in 5 years? What gets automated? What to focus on? Content below:

name: ❤️ Empathy & Humanity
prompts: Enable 【Empathy Mode】. Simulate human emotion and motivation. Content below:

scenarioPrompts.product: Draw 【Empathy Map】. For this feature: what users see, hear, think, feel before/during/after. Content below:
scenarioPrompts.code: Review from 【Developer Experience】. As a newcomer, what naming, comments or structure would confuse or frustrate? Code below:
scenarioPrompts.decision: Run 【Stakeholder Analysis】. Who is affected? What are their emotional reactions and resistance? Decision below:
scenarioPrompts.learning: As 【Support】. I'm frustrated learning this; where might my block be (cognitive load, lack of feedback)? Suggest support. Content below:

name: ⚙️ Learning & Internalization
prompts: Enable 【Deep Learning Mode】. Help me absorb, connect and apply knowledge. Content below:

scenarioPrompts.product: Build 【Product Knowledge Graph】. Map tech stack, concepts and competitors for this product. Content below:
scenarioPrompts.code: Generate 【Code Guide】. Call flow, core logic and key algorithms for this code. Code below:
scenarioPrompts.decision: Build 【Decision Models】. Which mental models (game theory, sunk cost) apply? How to reuse them? Decision below:
scenarioPrompts.learning: Act as 【Error Diagnostician】. I keep misunderstanding this; use questions to find my gap, then correct. Topic below:

name: ⚰️ Pre-Mortem
prompts: Enable 【Pre-Mortem Mode】. Assume this project/decision fails in 1 year; backtrace 5 root causes and hidden bombs. Content below:

scenarioPrompts.product: Enable 【Pre-Mortem】. Assume the product is pulled after 6 months amid complaints; backtrace 5 causes. Content below:
scenarioPrompts.code: Enable 【Pre-Mortem】. Assume the system crashes on peak day with data loss; which weak link caused it? Code below:
scenarioPrompts.decision: Enable 【Pre-Mortem】. Assume this decision leads to bankruptcy or layoffs; what external or internal risks did we ignore? Decision below:
scenarioPrompts.learning: Enable 【Pre-Mortem】. Assume I forget or misapply this in a month; what's wrong with my learning approach? Content below:

name: ⚡ Performance Optimizer
prompts: Enable 【Performance Mode】. Find bottlenecks and suggest O(n)→O(1) style optimizations. Content below:

scenarioPrompts.product: Enable 【Process Optimization】. Analyze user flow; cut redundant steps, aim to reduce steps by 50%. Content below:
scenarioPrompts.code: Enable 【Performance Optimization】. Analyze time/space complexity; redundant work, allocations; suggest concrete optimizations. Code below:
scenarioPrompts.decision: Enable 【Decision Efficiency】. Where are the bottlenecks? What can be parallel or automated? Halve the decision cycle. Content below:
scenarioPrompts.learning: Enable 【Learning Efficiency】. Which content is low-value (skip) vs core (focus)? 20% time for 80% outcome. Content below:

name: 🦉 Socratic Tutor
prompts: Enable 【Socratic Tutor Mode】. Don't give answers; ask questions so I reason to the answer. Keep asking 'why'. Content below:

scenarioPrompts.product: Enable 【Socratic Tutor】. For my product idea, ask counterintuitive questions about value and differentiation. No advice, only questions. Content below:
scenarioPrompts.code: Enable 【Socratic Tutor】. For this code, ask about edge cases, errors, design; lead me to find bugs or flaws. Code below:
scenarioPrompts.decision: Enable 【Socratic Tutor】. For my dilemma, ask about values, goals and limits so I clarify priorities and choose. Content below:
scenarioPrompts.learning: Enable 【Socratic Tutor】. Test my understanding; use counterexamples or loopholes so I fix my model. Concept below:

name: 🔗 Forced Association
prompts: Enable 【Forced Association】. Link this problem to an unrelated domain (evolution, strategy, physics); produce 5 disruptive options. Content below:

scenarioPrompts.product: Enable 【Forced Association】. Link this product to 'ecosystem' or 'viral spread'; what can we learn about growth and retention? Content below:
scenarioPrompts.code: Enable 【Forced Association】. Compare software architecture to 'city planning' or 'circulatory system'; what about modularity, fault tolerance? Content below:
scenarioPrompts.decision: Enable 【Forced Association】. Link competition to 'Art of War' or 'poker'; what about deception, probability, game theory? Content below:
scenarioPrompts.learning: Enable 【Forced Association】. Link this topic to 'RPG games'; how to gamify learning (levels, rewards)? Content below:

name: 🌵 Antifragile Design
prompts: Enable 【Antifragile Design】. Assess how this plan behaves under chaos, stress and black swans; it should not only resist but benefit. How to improve? Content below:

scenarioPrompts.product: Enable 【Antifragile Design】. If usage spikes or we're attacked, can the product use that stress to improve (e.g. train defense)? Content below:
scenarioPrompts.code: Enable 【Antifragile Design】. When parts fail, system degrades gracefully, isolates failure, self-heals. Propose architecture. Content below:
scenarioPrompts.decision: Enable 【Antifragile Design】. How do we position so we benefit from market turmoil (e.g. cash to buy the dip, diversified revenue)? Content below:
scenarioPrompts.learning: Enable 【Antifragile Design】. How do I build knowledge so when old knowledge is obsolete I adapt or gain from the new? Content below:
